title: umbraria microphylla
intro: Umbraria microphylla, also known as Indian snakeroot or Ageratina altissima, is a perennial plant native to Eastern North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to relax smooth muscles, reduce inflammation, and act as a sedative. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety, promoting relaxation, and aiding in the treatment of insomnia and other sleep disorders. Umbraria microphylla can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its herbal benefits, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a medicinal treatment.
